workflow.selectTokenHelper1: The results of this Processor are assigned to 2 attributes

workflow.checkMarketcapHelper1: This Processor will return marketcap and token price assigned to 2 variables

workflow.outputTokenAddressPlaceholder: Address of the token you want to receive after the swap

workflow.useNativeTokenAsOutput: Get native token after swap (if Output token is wrap token)?

workflow.totalTransactionTooltip: For example, if Total Transactions = 100, this Processor will broadcast 100 transactions at the same time, with different nonce. So all 100 transactions will be mined at the same block if possible
workflow.nodeNeedRunInCampaign: This Processor can only be used inside Campaigns

workflow.jsCodeToAnalyzeVariableHelper: Your code must have at least one return statement

workflow.startBlockTooltip: For example: if Start block = 100, the Contract sniper will start from block number 100
workflow.endBlockTooltip: For example: if End block = 200, the Contract sniper will stop at block number 200. If End block = 0, Contract sniper will not stop but will always run to catch the latest block

workflow.selectedChainInstruction: Name of selected chain, created from Processor
workflow.kyberIncludedDex: DEX IDs included in the route, separated by comma
workflow.kyberExcludedDex: DEX IDs excluded from the route, separated by comma

workflow.variableToSaveTransactionHashPl: Enter variable to save transaction hash
workflow.variableToSaveTokenAddressPlace: Enter variable to save token address

workflow.vanityPrefixPlaceholder: Enter prefix of the vanity address
workflow.variableToSavePrivateKeyPlaceho: Enter variable to save private key
workflow.pumpVanityAddressHelper: To create token address ending with 'pump', pre-generate a vanity address with that suffix and use its private key here.
workflow.vanityAddressPrivateKeyPlacehol: Enter private key of vanity address

workflow.openAIApiKeyTooltip: OpenAI API key is required for the AI Agent page and the AI Agent processor in Workflow
workflow.anthropicApiKeyTooltip: Anthropic API key is required for the AI Agent page and the AI Agent processor in Workflow
workflow.googleGeminiApiKeyTooltip: Google Gemini API key is required for the AI Agent page and the AI Agent processor in Workflow
workflow.openAIModelTooltip: Model to use with OpenAI (e.g. gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o). Leave empty for default
workflow.anthropicModelTooltip: Model to use with Anthropic (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6). Leave empty for default
workflow.googleGeminiModelTooltip: Model to use with Google Gemini (e.g. gemini-2.0-flash). Leave empty for default
workflow.backgroundModelTooltip: Model used for background tasks (memory extraction, conversation summary). Leave empty to use the main model. Recommended: a smaller, cheaper model (e.g. gpt-4o-mini, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, gemini-2.0-flash)
workflow.tavilyApiKeyTooltip: Tavily API key enables the AI Agent to search the web for current information and facts. Get a free key at tavily.com
workflow.exaApiKeyTooltip: Exa API key enables the AI Agent to perform semantic web search for research and finding related content. Get a free key at exa.ai
workflow.jupiterApiKeyTooltip: Jupiter API key is required for the AI Agent swap feature and the Jupiter swap processor in Workflow. Multiple API keys can be entered, separated by commas, and will be rotated in round-robin fashion
workflow.elementContentPlaceholder: Enter text content inside the element to click
workflow.debate.topicPlaceholder: Should we buy or sell SOL given the current market conditions?
workflow.debate.agentAPersonaPlaceholder: You are a bull investor. Argue why we should BUY.
workflow.debate.agentBPersonaPlaceholder: You are a bear investor. Argue why we should SELL.
workflow.debate.roundsHint: Select rounds · 1 round = Agent A argues, then Agent B responds

setting.screenCaptureProtectionTooltip: Blocks other apps from capturing or recording this app's screen
setting.importDatabaseHelpText: Note: All existing data of the app will be overwritten with new data
setting.selectFolderExportPlaceholder: Select the folder to export file
setting.exportHelpText: The exported file will be named
setting.telegramHelper: To run Campaign from Telegram, each device needs a different Device ID
setting.maxConcurrentWorkflowTooltip: Workflows in the Schedule can run simultaneously. You can customize the maximum number of Workflows that can run at the same time to suit your computer configuration or you can use the current default value
mcp.tokenCreatedNote: Store this token safely, it cannot be recovered once closed
mcp.deleteTokenConfirm: Delete this token? Active connections using it will be disconnected
mcp.howToConnect: Add the following to your MCP client config
extension.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete?
label.name: Tag name
alert.canImportMultipleFile: You can import multiple files at the same time
select.featureWantUse: Select the features you want to use
nodeProvider.createNodeEndpointHelper: You can refer to the list of free Node providers
nodeProvider.placeholderCreate: You can enter multiple endpoints and separate them by commas
topbar.needToDownloadBrowser: To run workflows, please download the Chrome browser first
tour.downloadBrowserDescription: If the latest browser version is already downloaded, it will be skipped
tour.telegramSetting: Only you have the right to create a bot and view bot information, KeeperAgent does not save any bot-related information on our server.
guide.openUrl.openNewTab: Opens a new URL in the current browser window.
guide.openUrl.example: For example: When you want to open a website
guide.openUrl.openAnyWeb: or any other website, you can use this processor.
guide.switchWindow.desc1: In most cases, the browser will have two types of windows: Main window and Popup window (Extension's popup window). By default, when running the workflow, the system will select the Main window. When you want to switch to the Popup window to operate, you can use this processor. Similarly, when you are in the Popup window and want to return to the Main window, you can use this processor.
guide.switchWindow.desc2: This processor is commonly used when you need to interact with browser extensions, for example when you need to: click Connect, Switch Network, Sign, Approve, Confirm, Reject, etc. in Metamask.
guide.typeText.title: Enters text into form fields.
guide.typeText.desc: For example, when logging into Twitter, you need to enter your username and password. You can use this processor to enter content into the login form.
guide.click.title: Clicks on a button or an element.
guide.click.desc: For example, when you have finished entering your username and password in the Twitter login form, you can use this processor to click on the "Sign in" button to complete the login.
guide.newTab.title: A browser's main window can have many tabs. Normally, running a workflow only requires one tab (the first tab is also the default tab). In special cases, if you want to open a new tab, you can use this processor.
guide.selectTab.title: When opening multiple tabs, you can use this processor to select the tab you want to use, just like clicking on a tab to switch to it.
guide.closeTab.title: Closes a tab that you no longer need.
guide.goBack.title: Returns to the previously visited website.
guide.reload.title: Reloads the current web page.
guide.scroll.title: Simulates scrolling the mouse up or down, or corresponding gestures when using the Touch Pad on macOS.
guide.note.title: Adds notes and explanations for certain parts of the workflow.
guide.crawl.title: Extracts data from a website.
guide.saveWallet.saveWallet: Saves wallet information to the database.
guide.saveWallet.example: For example: You can create a workflow that interacts with a Sui wallet to create new Sui wallets. Once you've created the wallet, you can use the Crawl Text processor to get the value of the seed phrase. Finally, you can use this processor to save the wallet you just created in the database on your computer.
guide.resource.title: Similar to the Save Wallet processor. This processor saves data to the database on your computer. This data will be managed on the Resources page.
guide.getGasPrice.title: Retrieves the current gas price of the blockchain. You need a Node provider to use this functionality.
guide.getGasPrice.example: For example: When you want to get the current gas price on Ethereum, you can use this processor. The gas price value obtained will correspond to the average gas price on the website: https://etherscan.io/gastracker
guide.getWalletBalance.title: Retrieves the balance of any wallet on the blockchain. You can get the balance in native tokens, ERC20 tokens, or ERC721 (or ERC1155) NFT tokens. You need a Node provider to use this functionality.
guide.getWalletBalance.example: For example: You can use this processor to check the ETH (native token) balance of a wallet on the Ethereum network, or check the Chainlink (LINK) token balance (ERC20 token) on the Ethereum network.
guide.importMetamask.title: Imports a wallet into Metamask. If your workflow needs to interact with the Metamask extension, the first thing you need to do is import the wallet into Metamask. This processor is usually the first processor in a workflow. By simply dragging and dropping, you can complete the wallet import operation (enter seed phrase, enter password, click Import, etc.).
guide.unlockMetamask.title: This processor helps automate the operation of unlocking Metamask by entering the password and clicking the "Unlock" button.
guide.connectMetamask.title: This processor helps automate the operation of pressing the "Connect" button on Metamask.
guide.confirmMetamask.title: This processor helps you click the "Confirm" button on Metamask.
guide.approveMetamask.title: This processor helps you click the "Approve" button on Metamask.
guide.cancelMetamask.title: This processor helps you click the "Cancel" button on Metamask.
guide.loginTwitter.title: Logs in to Twitter. With just a simple drag and drop operation, you can automate Twitter login.
guide.workflowType.title: Repeats a certain workflow. You can configure the number of repetitions as needed.
guide.generateProfile.title: This processor is often used while you are editing the workflow, helping you create profile tests to see how the workflow runs. When running in production, you should create a Campaign and select the profiles you want to run (these profiles are managed on the Profile page).
guide.generateProfile.example: For example: Your workflow needs to use both Metamask and Twitter. If you have a Profile, you will have information about Metamask wallet, Twitter Username and Password. After you have finished testing and see that the workflow runs well, you can delete the "Create profile" Processor and create a Campaign, select Scenario, Profile, Extension, Proxy to run the workflow you just tested.
guide.setAttribute.title: Each profile has attributes. If you want to change or add a new attribute, you can use this processor.
guide.solveCaptcha.title: Solves captchas such as reCAPTCHA and hCaptcha. With just a drag and drop operation, you can automatically solve these types of captchas.
guide.solveCaptcha.example: You need 2Captcha's API key to use this processor.
guide.getRandomValue.title: To avoid wallets being detected with identical operations, you should use this processor to generate random values.
guide.getRandomValue.example: When you want to swap for multiple wallets, instead of swapping a fixed amount for all wallets, you can swap a random value from 5 -> to 6, for example 5.1 USDT, 5.3 USDT, 5.8 USDT . Or you can randomly choose a token to swap instead of the fixed USDT, you can choose one of the following tokens to swap USDT, USDT, DAI, ...
guide.checkCondition.title: Stops or continues running the workflow based on a specific condition.
guide.checkCondition.example: This processor can be used in many different situations. For example, when you want to swap from USDT to ETH, you only want to run the workflow with profiles whose wallets have more than 100 USDT. You can use this processor to filter profiles, and wallets with a balance of less than 100 USDT will be ignored.
guide.elementExist.title: Checks whether an element exists on the web page.
guide.elementExist.example: For example: Before swapping on a DEX (KyberSwap, Uniswap, etc.), if you have not approved yet, the DEX will ask you to approve the token before swapping. You can use this processor to check whether there is an 'Approve' button or not. If so, click 'Approve' first, then click 'Swap' after approval is complete.
guide.clickExtension.title: Clicks an element inside a browser extension popup by matching its text content.
guide.clickExtension.desc: For example: To click the 'Approve' button inside the Metamask extension popup, enter 'Approve' as the element content. The processor will wait for the element to appear and click it automatically.
guide.uploadFile.title: Uploads a file by clicking the trigger element and accepting the file chooser dialog.
guide.uploadFile.desc: For example: To upload a profile picture, enter the CSS selector of the upload button (e.g. #upload-btn) and the absolute path to the file (e.g. /home/user/photo.png). The processor will click the button, intercept the file chooser, and submit the file automatically.
guide.randomOnOff.title: Randomly stops or continues running the workflow.
guide.randomOnOff.example: For example, to avoid wallets acting identically and being marked as Sybil, you can choose 70 wallets out of 100 (70% On) random wallets to run the workflow.
guide.importRabby.title: Imports a wallet into Rabby. If your workflow needs to interact with the Rabby extension, the first thing you need to do is import the wallet. This processor is usually the first processor in a workflow. By simply dragging and dropping, you can complete the wallet import operation (enter seed phrase, enter password, click Import, etc.).
guide.unlockRabby.title: Unlocks the Rabby wallet by entering the password and clicking the "Unlock" button.
guide.connectRabby.title: This processor helps you click the "Connect" button on the Rabby extension.
guide.cancelRabby.title: This processor helps you click the "Cancel" button on Rabby extension.
guide.signAndSubmit.title: This processor helps automate the operation of pressing the "Sign and Submit" and "Confirm" button on the Rabby wallet.
guide.addNetworkRabby.title: Adds a custom network to Rabby wallet. This processor is used to add network configuration for blockchains that Rabby does not yet support.
guide.calculate.title: Performs mathematical calculations: addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
guide.calculate.example: For example: In your wallet you have 1ETH, you want to swap all ETH into USDT but still want to keep 0.003ETH to pay gas fees. You can use this processor to calculate the amount of ETH you want to swap = 1 - 0.003 = 0.997 ETH.
guide.checkpoint.title: Persists a workflow variable to storage so its value is retained across workflow runs.
guide.checkpoint.example: For example: your workflow swaps ETH across 100 wallets. Place a Checkpoint node on the success path to save TOTAL_SWAP_SUCCESS = {{TOTAL_SWAP_SUCCESS}} + 1, and another on the error path to save TOTAL_SWAP_ERROR = {{TOTAL_SWAP_ERROR}} + 1. After every run, TOTAL_SWAP_SUCCESS and TOTAL_SWAP_ERROR accumulate across runs — giving you a live count of how many swaps succeeded or failed over time without needing to read logs.
guide.saveHistory.title: Saves the execution history of each profile.
guide.saveHistory.example: For example: When a wallet runs through many Workflows: Swap on Uniswap, Swap on SyncSwap, ... you should save the running history of the wallet, Key will be the wallet address (WALLET_ADDRESS), Tag will be the Workflow name: Swap on Uniswap, Swap on SyncSwap. When the activity history of the wallets is fully saved, you can analyze the wallets, see which wallets are less active, which wallets are more active, and can know which wallets have similar activities and Are they likely to be detected as Sybil or not
guide.importMartian.title: Imports a wallet into Martian wallet. If your workflow needs to interact with the Martian wallet extension, the first thing you need to do is import the wallet into Martian wallet. This processor is usually the first processor in a workflow. By simply dragging and dropping, you can complete the wallet import operation (enter seed phrase, enter password, click Import, etc.).
guide.unlockMartian.title: Unlocks the Martian wallet by entering the password and clicking the "Unlock" button.
guide.approveMartian.title: This processor helps you click the "Approve" button on the Martian wallet extension.
guide.switchMartian.title: This processor helps you click the "Switch" button on the Martian wallet extension.
guide.importPhantom.title: Imports a wallet into Phantom wallet. If your workflow needs to interact with the Phantom wallet extension, the first thing you need to do is import the wallet into Phantom wallet. This processor is usually the first processor in a workflow. By simply dragging and dropping, you can complete the wallet import operation (enter seed phrase, enter password, click Import, etc.).
guide.unlockPhantom.title: Unlocks the Phantom wallet by entering the password and clicking the "Unlock" button.
guide.selectToken.title: Selects tokens in the wallet with a balance greater than a certain threshold.
guide.selectToken.example: For example: you want to swap from USDC, USDT, or DAI to ETH with the condition that the selected token balance is greater than $10. You can use this Processor to randomly select a USDC or USDT or DAI token as long as the balance of the selected token is greater than $10.
guide.selectChain.title: Selects a chain in the wallet with a balance greater than a certain threshold. Similar to the "Select Token" processor, but the "Select Chain" processor can select tokens on many different EVM chains. This processor is suitable for operations that require interaction between multiple chains, such as bridging tokens or cross-chain swaps.
guide.selectChain.example: For example: When bridging tokens via Stargate Finance, you need to select Source chain, select token. You can use this Processor to check which chain you can choose, which token like USDT on Arbitrum, or USDC on Optimism, .... This Processor will return the selected token, along with the name of the selected chain.
guide.followTwitter.title: Follows or unfollows a Twitter account.
guide.likeTweet.title: Likes or unlikes a tweet.
guide.reTweet.title: Retweets or undoes a retweet of a tweet.
guide.replyTweet.title: Adds a comment or reply to a tweet.
guide.sendTelegram.title: Sends messages using a Telegram bot.
guide.updateProfile.title: Updates profile sub-column data.
guide.executeCode.title: Runs JavaScript code in the browser's Console dev tool or runs it independently without a browser.
guide.offOffProfile.title: Activates or deactivates a profile under certain conditions.
guide.connectPhantom.title: This processor helps you click the "Connect" button on the Phantom extension.
guide.confirmPhantom.title: This processor helps you click the "Confirm" button on the Phantom extension.
guide.readContract.title: Reads data directly from any smart contract. Data is read by calling the smart contract's function, similar to the 'Read contract' function on blockchain explorers such as Etherscan, BscScan, ArbitrumScan, etc.
guide.readContract.example: This processor only supports EVM chains such as Ethereum, BNB chain, Base, Arbitrum, ZKSync, ...
guide.writeToContract.title: Writes data to a smart contract by calling the smart contract's function, similar to the 'Write contract' function on blockchain explorers such as Etherscan, BscScan, ArbitrumScan, etc.
guide.writeToContract.example: This processor only supports EVM chains such as Ethereum, BNB chain, Base, Arbitrum, ZKSync, ...
guide.convertDecimal.title: Converts raw token amount to human-readable token amount using the formula: READABLE_AMOUNT = RAW_AMOUNT / Math.pow(10, TOKEN_DECIMALS).
guide.getTokenPrice.title: Retrieves token prices from CoinGecko or Dexscreener. You can get token prices from CEX (centralized exchanges) or DEX (decentralized exchanges) using the token address.
guide.checkTokenPrice.title: Checks token prices from CoinGecko or Dexscreener. This processor helps you check if the price is higher or lower than a certain value within a specific timeframe.
guide.checkMarketcap.title: Checks the market cap of a token from CoinGecko or Dexscreener. This processor helps you check if the market cap is higher or lower than a certain value within a specific timeframe.
guide.transferToken.title: Sends tokens between wallets. Supports sending native tokens such as ETH, BNB, APT, SUI, SOL, etc., or other tokens on EVM chains, Solana, Sui, and Aptos.
guide.approveToken.title: Creates an approval transaction for an ERC token. This processor only supports EVM chains.
guide.contractSniper.title: Tracks smart contracts via events. This processor only supports EVM chains.
guide.contractSniper.example: This processor allows you to build many different features as long as you know the ABI of the smart contract. For example: track transactions to receive and send tokens; track newly created Uniswap pools; track buy and sell orders of tokens on DEX...
guide.checkWallet.title: Checks if a wallet is saved in the database. Can search by wallet address, seed phrase, or private key.
guide.checkResource.title: Checks if a resource is saved in the database. Can search by any of the 10 columns of the resource.
guide.httpRequest.title: Calls an API using GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE methods. This processor supports custom JavaScript code to parse the API response.
guide.launchTokenPumpfun.desc: Launches a new token on the Pump.fun platform on the Solana blockchain. You only need to enter token name, symbol, image url, token will launch automatically within seconds
guide.launchTokenBonkfun.desc: Launches a new token on the Bonk.fun platform on the Solana blockchain. You only need to enter token name, symbol, image url, token will launch automatically within seconds
guide.askAgent.desc: Asks the AI agent a question. The agent will answer the question in text format using OpenAI's model. OpenAI's API key is required to use this processor.
guide.generateImage.desc: Generates an image using OpenAI (DALL-E) or Google (Imagen / Nano Banana). Select your provider, enter the corresponding API key, and configure the size or aspect ratio. The file path of the saved image is stored in the output variable.
guide.runAgent.desc: Runs a saved agent profile as a workflow step. The agent has access to all its configured tools, memory, MCP servers, and skills. Use {{variable}} in the prompt template to pass values from previous nodes. The agent's response is stored in the output variable for use in downstream nodes.
guide.debate.desc: Runs a structured debate between two AI agents on a given topic. Agent A and Agent B take turns arguing their positions over the configured number of rounds — each agent sees the full debate history before making its argument. After all rounds complete, a judge agent reviews the transcript and delivers an impartial verdict. The output variable stores either the verdict alone or the full transcript plus verdict depending on your output setting.
guide.swapJupiter.desc: Swaps any tokens on Solana using the Jupiter aggregator. This processor can broadcast multiple transactions simultaneously. To use this processor, you need to set Jupiter API in the Settings page..
guide.swapKyberswap.desc: Swaps any tokens on EVM chain like Ethereum, BNB chain, Base, Arbitrum, ZKSync, ... using the Kyberswap aggregator. This processor can broadcast multiple transactions simultaneously.
guide.swapPancakeswap.desc: Swaps tokens using the PancakeSwap aggregator on EVM blockchains.
guide.swapUniswap.title: Swaps tokens in a specific pool of Uniswap V2, Uniswap V3, or Uniswap V4. Swap transactions are performed via Uniswap's Universal Router, identical to swapping tokens via the Uniswap interface. This processor only supports EVM chains.
guide.swapUniswap.example: Compared to swapping tokens directly on the Uniswap interface, this processor helps you swap tokens faster because you don't have to go through the interface and don't need to interact with Metamask or Rabby extensions. In addition, this processor can perform multiple transactions simultaneously. If you enter 'Total transactions' greater than 1, for example 10, and each transaction swaps $30 worth of ETH to token A, this processor will create 10 transactions and broadcast them at the same time. All 10 transactions will be mined in the same block if possible, with a total value of $300.
guide.swapPancakeswap.title: Swaps tokens in a specific pool of PancakeSwap V2, PancakeSwap V3, or PancakeSwap Infinity. Swap transactions are performed via PancakeSwap's Universal Router, identical to swapping tokens via the PancakeSwap interface. This processor only supports EVM chains.
guide.swapPancakeswap.example: Compared to swapping tokens directly on the PancakeSwap interface, this processor helps you swap tokens faster because you don't have to go through the interface and don't need to interact with Metamask or Rabby extensions. In addition, this processor can perform multiple transactions simultaneously. If you enter 'Total transactions' greater than 1, for example 10, and each transaction swaps $30 worth of ETH to token A, this processor will create 10 transactions and broadcast them at the same time. All 10 transactions will be mined in the same block if possible, with a total value of $300.
guide.swapCetus.title: Swaps tokens in a specific pool of Cetus. Swap transactions are performed via Cetus's Router. This processor only supports the Sui blockchain.
guide.executeTransaction.title: Executes a transaction with transaction data. This processor only supports EVM chains.
guide.getPriorityFee.title: Retrieves the current priority fee on the Solana blockchain.
guide.generateVanityAddress.title: Generates a vanity address on the Solana blockchain. This processor helps you generate a vanity address with a specific prefix and suffix, such as a token address ending with 'pump', 'bonk', etc.
guide.snipeTelegram.title: Listens and retrieves messages from a specific Telegram chat.
guide.stopWorkflow.title: Forces the running workflow to stop when it is currently executing.
